//: React Native app — the Android build runs via ./gradlew / the react-native CLI, NOT the VSCode Java language server. We run that server in LightWeight mode so it never imports or builds the Gradle plugins under node_modules (@react-native/gradle-plugin, expo-*-gradle-plugin, @livekit/*-expo-plugin). In Standard/Hybrid mode it imported them and reported phantom 'Missing Gradle project configuration folder: .settings' / 'missing required library …shared-testutil.jar' / 'project cannot be built' errors that NEVER affected the real Gradle build. There is no Java application code in this repo (native code is Kotlin, handled by a separate extension), so LightWeight loses nothing here. Revert java.server.launchMode to 'Hybrid' if you ever need full Java project features.
